Getting Started

Get Tether up and running in under a minute. This guide covers installation, basic usage, and accessing your terminal from a mobile device.

Prerequisites

  • Rust 1.75+ — Install via rustup.rs
  • Linux or macOS — Windows support is experimental
  • A shell — bash, zsh, or any POSIX-compatible shell
  • A phone on the same WiFi — For mobile access

Installation

Option 1: From Source (Recommended)

git clone https://github.com/stanley-sujith-nelavala/tether.git cd tether cargo build --release

Option 2: Cargo Install

cargo install --git https://github.com/stanley-sujith-nelavala/tether.git

Option 3: Pre-built Binaries

Download compiled binaries from the Releases page.

Basic Usage

Start the Server

# Basic usage (localhost only) ./target/release/tether serve --password "mypassword" --allow-lan # Custom port ./target/release/tether serve --password "mypassword" --port 3000 --allow-lan

What Happens Next

When you start Tether, you'll see output like this:

INFO Tether server starting INFO Binding to 0.0.0.0:8080 INFO LAN IP: 192.168.1.100 INFO Access Tether at: http://192.168.1.100:8080 INFO QR Code: [QR code displayed in terminal]

Accessing from Your Phone

  1. Open the URL printed in the server output on your phone's browser
  2. Enter the password you set with --password
  3. Tap "New Terminal" to spawn your first shell
  4. Start typing — your laptop's terminal is now in your hand
💡 Tip: You can also scan the QR code printed in the server's terminal output to instantly open the URL on your phone.

CLI Reference

Flag Description Default
-p, --password Password for authentication required
-P, --port Port to bind to 8080
--allow-lan Allow LAN access (binds to 0.0.0.0) false
